subject: Ecology
title: Soil and Water
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Soil and Water.

hook.text: Soil is the planet's largest freshwater filter—every raindrop passes through it.

buildup.text: As water moves down, soil particles and microbes trap pollutants and break down contaminants.

discovery.text: Healthy soil absorbs rainfall like a sponge, reducing flood peaks and recharging groundwater.

twist.text: Degraded soil repels water instead of absorbing it—creating flash floods on bare hillsides.

climax.text: Restoring soil health is one of the most effective flood-prevention strategies available.

punchline.text: Healthy soil drinks rain. Sick soil rejects it.

quiz.question: How does healthy soil prevent flooding?
options: It absorbs rainfall like a sponge
It channels water into rivers faster
It freezes the water in place
